records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Equalized (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Equalizing (?).]

defs: To make equal; to cause to correspond, or be like, in amount or degree as compared; as, to equalize accounts, burdens, or taxes.
quotes: One poor moment can suffice To equalize the lofty and the low.
No system of instruction will completely equalize natural powers.

defs: To pronounce equal; to compare as equal.
quotes: Which we equalize, and perhaps would willingly prefer to the Iliad.

defs: To be equal to; equal; to match.
quotes: It could not equalize the hundredth part Of what her eyes have kindled in my heart.
